prompt: Please do the task stated in `/home/aaaaa/projects/codex_batch/re-arc/arc2_generation_prompts.md`, where the TASK_ID is `de493100`.
codex_response:
Implemented `de493100` end to end. The verifier recovers the masked rectangle with an ordered symmetry fallback: shifted vertical mirror first, then shifted horizontal mirror, then main-diagonal transpose. I treated the summary hint as a starting point but discarded its “single whole-image transform” reading; [notes.md](/home/aaaaa/projects/codex_batch/re-arc/arc2/tasks/task_de493100/notes.md) records the mismatch from official example 1.

The generator builds dense 30×30 symmetric kaleidoscope-style grids with the same fallback structure, then places a `7` rectangle in the same edge-biased family as the official task and rejects anything the verifier cannot reconstruct exactly. No new primitive was needed.

Verified:
- Official examples: `pass de493100: 5/5 official examples passed`
- Generated examples: `pass de493100: 1000/1000 generated examples passed`
- Preview inspected visually: [preview_01.png](/home/aaaaa/projects/codex_batch/re-arc/arc2/artifacts/de493100/previews/preview_01.png)
